title: rubus microphyllus var. incisus
intro: Rubus microphyllus var. incisus, also known as the dwarf raspberry or cut-leaf raspberry, is a variety of the Rubus microphyllus plant species. It is a shrub native to Asia, characterized by its small leaves and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of ellagic acid and other polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Rubus microphyllus var. incisus include reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and its herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, are used to support overall health and well-being.
